retry_reason: kobject_put releases a reference; retrying would risk a double-put (use-after-free) if the object's refcount already reached zero.
contract_summary: put wrapper that decrements the kobject refcount via kobject_put. After calling this function, the caller must not use the kobject pointer because it may have been freed. This function is commonly used as a resource cleanup callback (e.g., devm_add_action) to automatically release a kobject reference. No return value; the operation is unconditional and always succeeds (assuming the object is alive). Caller must ensure a valid reference exists before calling.
